heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 17 April 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 18 April 2026, is ranked 2nd among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 14 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 0 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (199°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 9.0°C) and outside temperature (down 8.4°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 23%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 24299 lux) and cloud levels (up 2744 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 18873 lux) and cloud levels (up 1631 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2740m lower than yesterday, 1630.7m lower than the past week, 436.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 2390m to a maximum of 8840m, averaging 2350m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 3980m and 2790m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; cold weather is expected. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and winds will be practically absent. Possible light showers, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (5.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
